subject: Chemistry
description: Master the fundamentals of electrolysis! This worksheet covers electrolysis of molten salts, aqueous concentrated and dilute solutions, products at electrodes, observations, half-equations, redox reactions, pH changes, and preferential discharge of ions. Show all working for calculations!

questions.question: Electrolysis is defined as the decomposition of an ionic compound using electricity. For electrolysis to occur, the ionic compound must be:
options: In molten state or dissolved in water (aqueous) to allow ions to move freely
questions.answer: In molten state or dissolved in water (aqueous) to allow ions to move freely

questions.question: In electrolysis, the electrode connected to the positive terminal of the power supply is called the:
During electrolysis, which statement correctly describes the movement of ions?
options: Ions do not move during electrolysis
Cations move to the cathode, anions move to the anode
All ions move to the cathode
Cations move to the anode, anions move to the cathode
questions.answer: Cations move to the cathode, anions move to the anode

questions.question:
At the cathode during electrolysis:

вЂў Cations gain electrons
вЂў Example: CuВІвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Cu

This process is called:

At the anode during electrolysis:

вЂў Anions lose electrons
вЂў Example: 2ClвЃ» в†’ Clв‚‚ + 2eвЃ»

This process is called:
When molten lead(II) bromide (PbBrв‚‚) is electrolysed, what ions are present in the molten salt?
options: PbВІвЃє, BrвЃ», HвЃє and OHвЃ» ions

questions.question:
During electrolysis of molten lead(II) bromide:

вЂў At cathode: PbВІвЃє ions gain electrons
вЂў At anode: BrвЃ» ions lose electrons

What is the correct half-equation at the CATHODE?
During electrolysis of molten lead(II) bromide, what is the correct half-equation at the ANODE?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 1: Electrolysis of Molten Salts - Observations
questions.question: During electrolysis of molten lead(II) bromide, what observation would you see at the CATHODE?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 1: Electrolysis of Molten Salts - Observations (continued)
questions.question: During electrolysis of molten lead(II) bromide, what observation would you see at the ANODE?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 1: Electrolysis of Molten Salts в­ђ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
Molten sodium chloride is electrolysed using inert electrodes.

Which row correctly shows the products at each electrode?

| Cathode | Anode |
|---------|-------|
| A: Chlorine | Sodium |
| B: Sodium | Chlorine |
| C: Hydrogen | Oxygen |
| D: Sodium | Oxygen |
options: D: Sodium at cathode, Oxygen at anode
A: Chlorine at cathode, Sodium at anode
C: Hydrogen at cathode, Oxygen at anode
B: Sodium at cathode, Chlorine at anode
questions.answer: B: Sodium at cathode, Chlorine at anode

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 2: Electrolysis of Aqueous Solutions - Introduction
questions.question: When an ionic compound is dissolved in water, additional ions are present due to the slight ionisation of water. What are these additional ions?

In aqueous solutions, there is competition between cations to be discharged at the cathode. The electrochemical series helps predict which cation is preferentially discharged.

Which statement is correct?
options: Cations lower in the electrochemical series are preferentially discharged
Only hydrogen ions are ever discharged
Cations higher in the electrochemical series are preferentially discharged
questions.answer: Cations lower in the electrochemical series are preferentially discharged

questions.question:
Given the electrochemical series (partial):
KвЃє > NaвЃє > CaВІвЃє > MgВІвЃє > AlВівЃє > ZnВІвЃє > FeВІвЃє > HвЃє > CuВІвЃє > AgвЃє

In an aqueous solution of sodium chloride, which cation will be preferentially discharged at the cathode?

At the anode, there is competition between anions. The general order of preferential discharge is:

SOв‚„ВІвЃ» < NOв‚ѓвЃ» < OHвЃ» < ClвЃ» < BrвЃ» < IвЃ»

Which anion is most easily discharged (loses electrons most readily)?

The concentration of ions also affects preferential discharge. In concentrated sodium chloride solution, chloride ions are discharged at the anode. In dilute sodium chloride solution, which ion is preferentially discharged at the anode?
In the electrolysis of CONCENTRATED aqueous sodium chloride (brine), what are the ions present in solution?

During electrolysis of CONCENTRATED sodium chloride solution:

вЂў At cathode: NaвЃє and HвЃє compete. HвЃє is lower in electrochemical series.
вЂў At anode: ClвЃ» and OHвЃ» compete. ClвЃ» is preferentially discharged when concentrated.

What are the products INITIALLY?
Write the half-equation for the reaction at the CATHODE during electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution.
Write the half-equation for the reaction at the ANODE during electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution.
During electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution, what observations would you see at the cathode?
options: Colourless gas evolved that gives a 'pop' sound with a lighted splint
Yellow-green gas with pungent smell evolved
questions.answer: Colourless gas evolved that gives a 'pop' sound with a lighted splint

questions.question: During electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution, what observations would you see at the anode?
options: Yellow-green gas with pungent/choking smell that bleaches damp litmus paper
Colourless gas that relights a glowing splint
questions.answer: Yellow-green gas with pungent/choking smell that bleaches damp litmus paper

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Concentrated NaCl - Products After Some Time
questions.question:
As electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride continues:
вЂў HвЃє ions are removed at cathode
вЂў ClвЃ» ions are removed at anode

After prolonged electrolysis, what happens to the concentration of ClвЃ» ions?
options: All chloride ions are immediately removed
Concentration of ClвЃ» decreases (solution becomes dilute)
questions.answer: Concentration of ClвЃ» decreases (solution becomes dilute)

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Concentrated NaCl - Products After Some Time (continued)
questions.question: When the sodium chloride solution becomes dilute after prolonged electrolysis, the product at the anode changes. What is produced at the anode in DILUTE sodium chloride solution?

During electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution:
вЂў HвЃє ions are discharged at cathode (removed from solution)
вЂў NaвЃє and OHвЃ» ions remain in solution

What happens to the pH of the solution after some time?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Concentrated NaCl в­ђ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: After prolonged electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution, the remaining solution becomes alkaline. What compound is formed in this solution and why?
options: Pure water - all ions are removed
Sodium hydroxide - because NaвЃє and OHвЃ» ions remain after HвЃє and ClвЃ» are discharged
Hydrochloric acid - because chlorine dissolves in water
questions.answer: Sodium hydroxide - because NaвЃє and OHвЃ» ions remain after HвЃє and ClвЃ» are discharged

questions.question: In the electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid (Hв‚‚SOв‚„), what ions are present in the solution?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Dilute Sulfuric Acid - Cathode Product
questions.question:
At the cathode during electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid:

HвЃє ions are the only cations that can be discharged (water provides HвЃє and OHвЃ»).

What is the product at the cathode?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Dilute Sulfuric Acid - Anode Product
questions.question:
At the anode during electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid:

SOв‚„ВІвЃ» and OHвЃ» compete. OHвЃ» is preferentially discharged over SOв‚„ВІвЃ».

What is the product at the anode?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Dilute Sulfuric Acid - Half Equations
questions.question: What is the correct half-equation at the ANODE during electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Dilute Sulfuric Acid - Volume Ratio
questions.question:
During electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid:

Cathode: 2HвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Hв‚‚
Anode: 4OHвЃ» в†’ Oв‚‚ + 2Hв‚‚O + 4eвЃ»

What is the ratio of volume of hydrogen to oxygen produced?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Dilute Sulfuric Acid - Concentration Changes
questions.question:
During electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid:
вЂў HвЃє ions are removed at cathode
вЂў OHвЃ» ions are removed at anode
вЂў HвЃє and OHвЃ» come from water

What happens to the concentration of sulfuric acid as electrolysis continues?
options: Concentration increases (water is removed, acid remains)
questions.answer: Concentration increases (water is removed, acid remains)

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Dilute Sulfuric Acid в­ђ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
In electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid, 4 moles of electrons flow through the circuit. 

At cathode: 2HвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Hв‚‚
At anode: 4OHвЃ» в†’ Oв‚‚ + 2Hв‚‚O + 4eвЃ»

How many moles of hydrogen and oxygen are produced?

Hint: 2 mol eвЃ» produces 1 mol Hв‚‚; 4 mol eвЃ» produces 1 mol Oв‚‚
In the electrolysis of aqueous copper(II) sulfate using inert (carbon/platinum) electrodes, what ions are present?

At the cathode during electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution:

CuВІвЃє and HвЃє compete. CuВІвЃє is lower than HвЃє in the electrochemical series.

What is discharged at the cathode?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 5: Copper(II) Sulfate - Cathode Half Equation
questions.question: What is the correct half-equation at the cathode during electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution?

At the anode during electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution with INERT electrodes:

SOв‚„ВІвЃ» and OHвЃ» compete. OHвЃ» is preferentially discharged.

What is produced at the anode?
During electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution with inert electrodes, what observation would you see at the cathode?
As electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution continues, CuВІвЃє ions are removed at the cathode. What happens to the colour of the solution?
options: Colour changes from blue to green
Blue colour fades and eventually becomes colourless
questions.answer: Blue colour fades and eventually becomes colourless

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 5: Copper(II) Sulfate - After Prolonged Electrolysis
questions.question: After prolonged electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution, all CuВІвЃє ions are discharged. What will then be discharged at the cathode?

During electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution:
вЂў OHвЃ» ions are discharged at anode
вЂў HвЃє ions remain in solution initially

What happens to the pH of the solution as electrolysis proceeds?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 5: Copper(II) Sulfate в­ђ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
During electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution, 0.1 mol of electrons pass through the circuit.

Cathode: CuВІвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Cu

How many moles of copper are deposited?

Hint: 2 mol of electrons deposit 1 mol of Cu

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Dilute Sodium Chloride vs Concentrated
questions.question:
Compare electrolysis of DILUTE and CONCENTRATED sodium chloride solution.

In DILUTE sodium chloride solution, what is produced at the anode?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Dilute Sodium Chloride - Half Equation at Anode
questions.question:
In dilute sodium chloride solution, OHвЃ» ions are preferentially discharged at the anode (not ClвЃ»).

What is the correct half-equation at the anode?
options: Both B and C are acceptable
questions.answer: Both B and C are acceptable

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Dilute Sodium Chloride - pH Change
questions.question:
During electrolysis of DILUTE sodium chloride solution:
вЂў At cathode: HвЃє is discharged
вЂў At anode: OHвЃ» is discharged

Since equal amounts of HвЃє and OHвЃ» are removed, what happens to the pH?

During electrolysis of molten zinc chloride:

Cathode: ZnВІвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Zn

If 4 moles of electrons pass through the circuit, how many moles of zinc are deposited?

During electrolysis:

Anode: 2BrвЃ» в†’ Brв‚‚ + 2eвЃ»

If 0.5 mol of bromine (Brв‚‚) is produced, how many moles of electrons were released?

In electrolysis, the charge of an ion determines how many electrons are needed to discharge it.

For AlВівЃє + 3eвЃ» в†’ Al

If 6 moles of electrons pass through molten aluminium oxide, how many moles of aluminium are produced?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 7: Mole Calculations в­ђ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
During electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid, 0.4 mol of hydrogen gas is produced at the cathode.

Cathode: 2HвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Hв‚‚
Anode: 4OHвЃ» в†’ Oв‚‚ + 2Hв‚‚O + 4eвЃ»

Calculate:
(a) Moles of electrons that passed = 0.4 Г— 2 = 0.8 mol
(b) Moles of oxygen produced at anode = ?

A student electrolyses concentrated copper(II) chloride solution using inert electrodes.

Ions present: CuВІвЃє, ClвЃ», HвЃє, OHвЃ»

Which row correctly identifies the products and the change in pH?

| Cathode | Anode | pH change |
|---------|-------|-----------|
options: Copper, Chlorine, pH remains unchanged or slightly increases
questions.answer: Copper, Chlorine, pH remains unchanged or slightly increases
